response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference 

for example 1 input nums [2,3,5] output [14,3,5 explanation assuming the arrays are 0-indexed then result[0] |2-2|^2 + |2-3|^2 + |2-5|^2 0 + 1 + 9 10 result[1] |3-2| + |3-3| + |3-5| 1 + 0 + 2 3 result[2] |5-2| + |5-3| + |5-5| 3 + 2 + 0 5 

*** 

the approach to solve this problem is to iterate through each element of the array and calculate the required sum based on the condition. for each element at index i, if i is a multiple of 3, calculate the sum of squares of the differences between nums[i] and all other elements. otherwise, calculate the sum of absolute differences. 

to efficiently compute the sum of absolute differences, we can use prefix sums. for a sorted array, the sum of absolute differences can be computed using the prefix sum array. for example, if the array is sorted, the sum of absolute differences for nums[i] is nums[i] * (i) - prefix_sum[i] + (n - 1 - i) * nums[n - 1] - prefix_sum[n - 1]. 

this approach ensures that the solution is efficient and runs in linear time, making it suitable for large input sizes up to 10^5.
